en: ’Awni, are you home?
Yes, come on in.
’Awni, are you home?

ab: i’ikay ki ’Awni! kake ka ’Ipolo, mani patoloiso? no kelayninga ka no patikaykay mekake ’Awniane.
ch: B:’Awni不在家耶!我是’Ipolo，有什麼事嗎? 回來我會轉告’Awni。
en: ’Awni is not at home. I am ‘Ipolo. What can I do for you? I will let him know when he comes back.
Who are you?
Grandma, we are Moini and ‘Awni, please open the door for us.
Who is it?

ab: kake ka Tanobake, ’awkay kake silape Lawcoane.
en: I am Tanobake, I am here for Lawcoane.
Grandma, I am here to see you.

ch: A:你好久沒來了，我好想念你。
en: You haven’t been here for a long time. I missed you so much.
’Awni, I am here to play with you.

ab: moda’ana, wa’adra’adraw kake mosoa ko booane.
en: Come on in! I have been waiting for you for a long time.

ab: kikay ka doko’ili na bwathi, kwana lwalaa.
ch: A:這是我種的地瓜，吃吃看!
en: This is the sweet potato I grow. Try it!
The sweet potato is so sweet.

ab: ngwalra! kikay ka doko’ili na madro, kwana lwalaa.
ch: A:過來!這是我種的水果，吃看看。
en: Come here! These are the fruits I grow. You should try it.
The fruit tastes sweet.

ab: ’inonwa, kikay ka doko’ili na i’iposabo’oane ka ngodrangodro, kwana lalaa.
ch: A:請坐! 這是我種的有機玉米吃看看!
en: Take a seat! You should try some organic corn that I grow.
Wow! The corn tastes so sweet.
Do you want some oranges?

ab: idoo kake kane na athiwko, kamani kane na lrin-go.
ch: B:我不能吃橘子，我吃蘋果就好。
en: I can’t eat oranges. I will have some apples instead.
Would you want some cookies?
Sure, I like it very much.

ab: ko kathape na madro laso kane.
en: Let me peel the fruit for you.
Thank you. This fruit is so juicy.

ab: ko alra na acilay pa’ongolo mosoa.
en: I will bring you some water.
Thank you.
Enjoy yourself. I cooked a lot of dishes.

ch: B:嗚! 真的很香也好吃。
en: Oh! It smells good and looks delicious.
Enjoy yourself. There is a lot more.
I’ll make myself at home then.
Enjoy yourself. There are plenty here.

ch: B:我吃了好多，不能再吃了。
en: I ate a lot and couldn’t eat any more.
